input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install **Xcode Command Line Tools** afterward:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  ```
- Open Xcode once, accept the license, and let it install platform components.
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs list minimum Xcode version per .NET release at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/). Match your .NET version to the required Xcode.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — grab the latest **LTS or STS** SDK (currently .NET 8 LTS or .NET 9).
- Use the `.pkg` installer for macOS (arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in all sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `maui-maccatalyst`, etc.
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet workload list          # should show "maui" installed
  ```

## 4. Install JDK (Android requirement)

The MAUI Android workload bundles a JDK, but if you need a standalone one:

- **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) (recommended) or Adoptium Temurin.
- **Required version:** JDK 17 (for current MAUI/.NET 8+).
- Set `JAVA_HOME`:
  ```bash
  export JAVA_HOME=$(/usr/libexec/java_home -v 17)
  ```

## 5. Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK. To manage it explicitly:

- SDKs land in `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/` by default.
- Or install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for the emulator and SDK Manager GUI.
- **Required components** (auto-installed by workload, verify in SDK Manager):
  - Android SDK Platform (API level matching your `TargetFramework`, e.g., API 34)
  - Android SDK Build-Tools
  - Android Emulator + a system image (or use a physical device)

## 6. IDE — pick one

| Option | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (sunset — not recommended for new setups) | — |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

VS Code with the **".NET MAUI" extension** is now the official recommended path on macOS.

## 7. Verify the full toolchain

```bash
# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

All four commands should succeed with no errors.

## 8. Troubleshooting checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `xcode-select: error` | Run `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Missing Android SDK | `dotnet workload repair` or set `ANDROID_HOME` |
| Workload version mismatch | `dotnet workload update` |
| iOS signing errors | Open Xcode → Preferences → Accounts, add Apple ID |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → (optionally Android Studio for emulator) → VS Code + MAUI extension → verify with `dotnet new maui`.
